User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account on the FreshStart Resume Builder platform. This entity stores basic user information required for session persistence and resume ownership.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: Email address of the user, used for authentication (in conjunction with an external system) and contact. Must be unique.

firstName.description: The first name of the user, used for personalization and resume PDF naming.

lastName.description: The last name of the user, used for personalization and resume PDF naming.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was last updated.

Resume.title: Resume
Resume.description: Represents a single resume document created by a user. This entity acts as a central manifest, holding references to all its constituent sections.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resume entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this resume. (Relationship: User 1:N Resume)

title.description: A user-defined title for the resume (e.g., 'My Software Engineer Resume').

templateId.description: Identifier for the resume template used. For v1, this will typically be a default ID (e.g., 'fresher-default-v1').

theme.description: The chosen accent color theme for the resume (e.g., 'blue', 'green', 'purple').

currentProgress.description: A numerical representation (0-100) of the resume's completion percentage.

personalInformationId.description: Reference to the PersonalInformation entity for this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:1 PersonalInformation)

professionalSummaryId.description: Reference to the ProfessionalSummary entity for this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:1 ProfessionalSummary)

educationEntryIds.description: References to EducationEntry entities associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N EducationEntry)

skills.description: A list of technical skills, tools, and languages associated with the resume, entered as tags. For v1, this is a simple list of strings.

projectIds.description: References to Project entities associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Project)

internshipExperienceIds.description: References to InternshipExperience entities associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N InternshipExperience)

certificationIds.description: References to Certification entities associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Certification)

achievementAwardIds.description: References to AchievementAward entities associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N AchievementAward)

extracurricularIds.description: References to Extracurricular entities associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Extracurricular)

languageProficiencyIds.description: References to LanguageProficiency entities associated with this resume. (Relationship: Resume 1:N LanguageProficiency)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the resume was first created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the resume was last updated.

PersonalInformation.title: PersonalInformation
PersonalInformation.description: Stores the personal contact and identification details (e.g., name, email, phone) for a specific resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PersonalInformation entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this personal information belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:1 PersonalInformation)

fullName.description: The full name of the resume owner.

email.description: The primary email address for contact.

phone.description: The primary phone number for contact.

linkedInUrl.description: URL to the user's LinkedIn profile.

gitHubUrl.description: URL to the user's GitHub profile.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this personal information record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this personal information record was last updated.

ProfessionalSummary.title: ProfessionalSummary
ProfessionalSummary.description: Stores the professional summary text for a specific resume. This section is designed for a concise overview of the user's career aspirations and qualifications.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ProfessionalSummary entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this professional summary belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:1 ProfessionalSummary)

content.description: The 2-3 sentence professional summary text, potentially refined by AI.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this professional summary record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this professional summary record was last updated.

EducationEntry.title: EducationEntry
EducationEntry.description: Represents a single educational qualification or degree listed on a resume. Users can add multiple education entries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EducationEntry entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this education entry belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N EducationEntry)

degree.description: The degree obtained or pursued (e.g., 'Bachelor of Technology', 'Master of Science').

institution.description: The name of the educational institution.

graduationYear.description: The year of graduation or expected graduation.

cgpa.description: Cumulative Grade Point Average (CGPA), GPA, or equivalent numerical score.

relevantCoursework.description: A list of relevant courses or subjects taken, provided as bullet points.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this education entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this education entry was last updated.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project experience listed on a resume. This entity stores details about personal, academic, or professional projects.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this project belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Project)

title.description: The title or name of the project.

description.description: A detailed description of the project, including scope, methodologies, and contributions.

techStack.description: A list of key technologies, tools, or programming languages used in the project.

gitHubUrl.description: URL to the project's GitHub repository or other relevant online portfolio/source code.

outcome.description: The measurable outcome, impact, or key achievement of the project.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this project entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this project entry was last updated.

InternshipExperience.title: InternshipExperience
InternshipExperience.description: Represents an internship or relevant work experience listed on a resume. Users can add multiple experience entries.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InternshipExperience entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this experience belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N InternshipExperience)

company.description: The name of the company or organization where the experience was gained.

role.description: The role or position held during the internship or experience.

duration.description: The duration of the experience, typically as a formatted string (e.g., 'Jan 2023 - Jun 2023' or 'May 2022 - Present').

responsibilities.description: A list of key responsibilities, duties, and achievements in bullet points.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this internship experience entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this internship experience entry was last updated.

Certification.title: Certification
Certification.description: Represents a professional certification or license obtained by the user, listed on a resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Certification entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this certification belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Certification)

name.description: The full name of the certification (e.g., 'AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner').

issuer.description: The issuing organization or body for the certification.

year.description: The year the certification was obtained.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this certification entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this certification entry was last updated.

AchievementAward.title: AchievementAward
AchievementAward.description: Represents a notable achievement or award received by the user, listed on a resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AchievementAward entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this achievement/award belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N AchievementAward)

description.description: A free-text description of the achievement or award, typically a bullet point.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this achievement/award entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this achievement/award entry was last updated.

Extracurricular.title: Extracurricular
Extracurricular.description: Represents an extracurricular activity, club membership, or volunteering experience listed on a resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Extracurricular entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this extracurricular activity belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Extracurricular)

description.description: A free-text description of the extracurricular activity (e.g., 'Member of Robotics Club', 'Volunteer at Local Shelter').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this extracurricular entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this extracurricular entry was last updated.

LanguageProficiency.title: LanguageProficiency
LanguageProficiency.description: Represents a language known by the user and their proficiency level, listed on a resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LanguageProficiency entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this language proficiency belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N LanguageProficiency)

proficiencyLevel.description: The proficiency level in the language (e.g., 'Native', 'Fluent', 'Intermediate', 'Basic').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this language proficiency entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this language proficiency entry was last updated.

definition.description: Stores core user profile information. The document ID `{userId}` is the Firebase Authentication UID. This acts as the root for all user-owned data.
Stores a single resume document, acting as a manifest for its constituent sections. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific resume.

definition.description: Stores the personal contact details for a specific resume. This is a singleton sub-document ('main'). Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'resumeId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent Resume.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the FreshStart Resume Builder platform is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Hierarchical Path-Based Access Modeling. The core principle driving this design is to ensure that security rules are simple, robust, and do not rely on costly and complex `get()` calls.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
To achieve critical authorization independence, the `userId` of the resume owner will be denormalized into every sub-document that logically belongs to a resume. This means that documents within subcollections like `personalInformation`, `educationEntries`, `projects`, etc., will not only contain their respective `resumeId` but also the `userId` of the owner. This allows security rules to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` on the specific document being accessed, eliminating the need to `get()` a parent `Resume` document or traverse up the hierarchy. This significantly simplifies rule writing, improves performance, and enables atomic operations like creating a new resume with its initial personal information in a single batched write, as rules can validate each document independently.

**Structural Segregation & Hierarchical Access Modeling:**
All user-specific data, including their resumes and all associated sections, are strictly segregated under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This enforces a homogeneous security posture: only the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`) can access or modify their own data. Within this, each resume gets its own subcollection: `/users/{userId}/resumes/{resumeId}`. This path directly embeds the `userId`, which means any attempt to query or access data for a different user's path will be immediately denied by security rules (e.g., `match /users/{userId}/{documents=**} { allow read, write: if request.auth.uid == userId; }`).

For sections that have a 1:1 relationship with a `Resume` (like `PersonalInformation` and `ProfessionalSummary`), they are placed in subcollections with a fixed document ID (e.g., `/personalInformation/main`). This clearly signals that there is only one such document per resume, simplifying client-side data fetching and rule matching. For sections with a 1:N relationship (e.g., `EducationEntry`, `Project`), they reside in their own subcollections (e.g., `/educationEntries/{educationEntryId}`). This structure ensures that listing operations (QAPs) are secure: a user can only list their own `educationEntries` for a specific `resumeId` under their `userId` path, and each document returned will further be validated to contain the correct `userId` and `resumeId` in its data.

This hierarchical, path-based ownership combined with strategic denormalization directly supports the QAP principle by ensuring that any `list` operation within a user's `resumes` path, or any of its subcollections, will automatically be constrained to the authenticated user's data without requiring complex query filters in security rules.
